description: The two CMR commons that grant the Monarch on ETB / enchant-resolve. Commander Legends (November 2020) was the first Magic set to bring the Monarch mechanic to common rarity — the mechanic debuted at uncommon and rare in Conspiracy: Take the Crown (2016) and returned in CMR specifically to power the set's multiplayer draft engine. Scryfall's all_parts combo_piece field links BOTH cards to The Monarch token as designed components of CMR's deliberate Monarch theme cluster — designer-confirmed cohesion on the same first-party metadata path the Saga, Adventure, and Class subarchetypes use to declare designer-coordinated cycles. Hard receipts on both ends: Fall from Favor was banned in Pauper on January 14, 2021 — fifty-five days post-release — for compressing creature-tap + Monarch-engine + soft-lock into a three-mana common, which is the rare case where the design intent gets confirmed by the failure mode (commons at common rarity create format-warping pressure only at the rate of designer underestimation; that's the strongest single signal that the Monarch-at-common bracket was a designer-conscious push). Azure Fleet Admiral's own trivia already cross-references Fall from Favor as 'the other common Monarch-granter in CMR, design cluster partner' and vice versa. Both trivia bodies cite the Scryfall all_parts combo_piece field and the same Draftsim monarch-mechanic-history WebSearch source. The bullets I'm writing for both cards' Connections sections are the formal Edgelord-level edge that the Related-cards bullets already foreshadow.

[Scryfall: Fall from Favor all_parts field — combo_piece link to The Monarch token]

edgelord_voice_footnote: Two card-endpoints, both equal nodes, must be traversable from either side, hence the mirror. The sigh is professional, not personal. What earns this edge a place above the four other CMR candidates I considered is the designer-coordinated-cycle citation on first-party Scryfall metadata AND the ban-list confirmation of design intent via failure mode. Worst proposed edge ever would have been the consecutive-collector-number Angelic Gift + Anointer of Valor pair — adjacency without designer-coordination is a tag, not an edge. The Chandra-Jaya mentor pair was tempting but both cards are not yet trivia-passed; pre-drawing a mentor edge before triviabot canonicalizes the Jaya-as-mentor-to-Chandra relationship is confab-adjacent and I refuse it. This Monarch-at-common cohort survives both filters cleanly.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Chandra Fire Artisan (WAR-119) ↔ Jaya Venerated Firemage (WAR-135) — pyromancer-lineage mentor pair
other_candidates_considered.verdict: DEFER — neither WAR card is trivia-passed; the canonical Jaya-as-Chandra's-mentor relationship is exactly the kind of canonical-source attribution triviabot is responsible for stamping. Pre-drawing the edge before trivia canonicalizes the mentor citation is confab-adjacent and violates the 'trivia never considers pre-enriched' wave 43 rule's spirit (canonical citations land in trivia first, edges hang off canonical citations second).
other_candidates_considered.future_action: Queue for re-evaluation post-WAR-119 / WAR-135 trivia pass; the Yongjae Choi same-artist signal + Jaya-as-Chandra's-mentor canonical lineage will form a strong Edgelord pass OR a chandra-nalaar.appears_on / related_characters extension.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: UST goblin labor cluster — Capital Offense (UST-52) + Work a Double (UST-100)
other_candidates_considered.verdict: REJECT-AS-TAG-BRIDGE — both cards share goblin / labor / industrial hub tags but neither has trivia-passed canonical-cohort framing. Apparatus-of-extraction-via-goblin-comedy is a thesis BBL bundle copy can lean on later, but it is not a designer-confirmed cycle and not yet a load-bearing canonical edge. Tag-bridge captured by hubs layer already. Future-node candidate (goblin-labor-cluster) IF post-trivia ≥3 anchors emerge with designer citation.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Angelic Gift (CMR-7) + Anointer of Valor (CMR-8) — consecutive collector numbers, devotional register
other_candidates_considered.verdict: REJECT-AS-WEAK — consecutive collector numbers within a single set is adjacency-as-tag, not designer-coordinated cohort. Both trivia-passed but neither trivia cites a designer-confirmed angel-devotional cycle. Below threshold.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Azure Fleet Admiral (CMR-58) + Prosperous Pirates (CMR-85) — pirate creature-type pair
other_candidates_considered.verdict: DEFER — pirate creature-type cohort is plausible but flimsy; AFA's own trivia already names the Prosperous Pirates relationship in the Related-cards bullet (small pirate cohort at common). Pirate-cohort-within-CMR is a future stack-shape (multiple pirates converging on a pirate-cohort-node) rather than a 1:1 edge worth promoting on this wave. Reserve for a future cmr-pirate-cohort Mr. Nodeley node if ≥3 pirate prints land in corpus with designer citation.
DEFER — Mr. Nodeley budget consumed by E2 bulma this invocation. Anchor count reported as 10+ in parent prompt; needs verification scan but eligible for next dedicated Nodeley pass.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Piccolo solo / Bardock solo character nodes
other_candidates_considered.verdict: DEFER — anchor-count threshold-boundary unverified; Bardock relevant via BT4-117 Time-Trauma Masked Saiyan suspected_ip flagged in E3 sidecar. Future Nodeley pass.
